retrieved_docs.title: Beta Thalassemia -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The CBC and peripheral smear findings are non-specific. A diagnosis of beta-thalassemia requires hemoglobin electrophoresis or high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) to demonstrate abnormal percentages of HbA, HbA2, and sometimes HbF. The general pattern of beta-thalassemia is a decreased HbA percentage and a mildly increased HbA2; less than 10% with variably increased HbF. A HbA2 above 10% suggests variant hemoglobin rather than beta-thalassemia. The magnitude of the HbA decrease depends on the genetic makeup of the affected individual. Patients with beta(+) alleles will have variably decreased HbA levels, and those that are homozygous beta(0) will produce no HbA. Beta-thalassemia minor characteristically has increased HbA2 (4-8%) with variably normal-to-low elevations of HbF. Beta-thalassemia major typically shows markedly elevated HbF (30-to-greater than 95%) with normal to mildly elevated HbA2. The distinction between beta-thalassemia major and intermedia is a clinical one and does not have diagnostic laboratory findings.

retrieved_docs.title: Beta Thalassemia Major (Cooley Anemia) -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Morphological changes of RBCs like microcytosis, hypochromia, poikilocytosis, anisocytosis, and nucleated RBCs can be seen on the peripheral blood smear. Additionally, target cells, teardrop cells, and basophilic stippling (course type) can also be visualized on the peripheral blood smear. The number of erythroblasts depends on the degree of anemia and after splenectomy. Moreover, either Hb electrophoresis or high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) is warranted for definitive diagnosis. It analyzes Hb both quantitatively and qualitatively. Beta-thalassemia major demonstrates a complete absence of beta-globin chain (beta0/beta0), therefore the HbF is 92% to 95%, HbA2 is 5% to 8%, and HbA 0%.

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Beta Thalassemia -- Results, Reporting, and Critical Findings -- Beta Thalassemia Major
retrieved_docs.content: They present early in life with severe normocytic/microcytic anemia. The hemoglobin level is usually less than 7 g/dL. The MCH is low, <20 pg, and peripheral smear show marked poikilocytosis with tear-drop cell, erythroblasts, and target cells. [3] In classical beta thalassemia major (B0 homozygotes), the hemoglobin analysis shows to be absent, and HbF constitutes 92 to 95% of the total hemoglobin. In major forms resulting from double heterozygosity of B0/B+, the HbA levels are between 10 and 30%, and HbF comprises 70 and 90% of total hemoglobin. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Alpha Thalassemia -- Results, Reporting, and Critical Findings
retrieved_docs.content: Healthy adults usually have the maximum amount of hemoglobin A (HbA), comprising 95% to 98% of their total blood hemoglobin. Variants like hemoglobin A 2 (HbA 2 ) constitute 2% to 3%, while hemoglobin F (HbF) consists of less than 2% of total adult hemoglobin. All α-thalassemia-affected individuals show varying degrees of anemia that may manifest as low hemoglobin, MCH, and MCV and normal to slightly decreased HbA 2 levels.

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Beta Thalassemia -- Results, Reporting, and Critical Findings
retrieved_docs.content: In normal adults, Hemoglobin A (HbA) makes up 95 to 98% of the total hemoglobin, hemoglobin A2 (HbA2) is 2 to 3% of hemoglobin, and hemoglobin F(HbF) makes up less than 2% of total adult hemoglobin. [3] An increased Hb A2 is present in heterozygous beta thalassemia; therefore, its precise measurement is essential for diagnosing or excluding the beta thalassemia trait.

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Alpha Thalassemia -- Results, Reporting, and Critical Findings -- α-Thalassemia Major (Hb Bart's Hydrops Fetalis Syndrome)
retrieved_docs.content: Laboratory investigation of the parents of fetuses with Hb Bart's hydrops fetalis shows a typical HPLC pattern with normal HbF and HbA 2 quantification. [44] Parental analysis typically shows a decreased hemoglobin concentration, MCH, and MCV. The blood smear also typically shows hypochromic, microcytic red cells (see Image . Peripheral Blood Smear for a Case of Hydrops Fetalis). The HbH inclusion body test is usually positive in the parents. [43]

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Beta Thalassemia -- Results, Reporting, and Critical Findings -- Beta Thalssemia Intermedia
retrieved_docs.content: They have mild-moderate anemia with hemoglobin, even in severe forms not going below 7 g/dL, ranging from 7 to 10 g/dL. In thalassemia intermedia, there is more than or equal to 3.5% HbA2, and HbF ranges between 10 to 50%. The molecular defect and the degree of erythropoiesis determine the amount of Hb A2 and Hb F. Molecular testing is a more accurate diagnostic tool. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Overview on Ordering and Evaluation of Laboratory Tests -- Diagnostic Tests -- Peripheral Smear
retrieved_docs.content: Peripheral smear gives information regarding abnormality in size, shape, color, counts, and composition (inclusions) of cells compared to normal.

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Beta Thalassemia -- Specimen Requirements and Procedure
retrieved_docs.content: The specimen collection varies with the sample chosen for testing. The diagnosis requires a combination of laboratory tests, including measuring red cell indices, Hb analysis, and quantifying HbF and HbA2. Whole blood is collected in EDTA vials, and samples are sent for a complete blood profile, electrophoresis, red cell indices, and molecular testing. [3] EDTA is the preferred anticoagulant, although other anticoagulants can also be used. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Alpha Thalassemia -- Results, Reporting, and Critical Findings -- α-Thalassemia Intermedia (HbH Disease)
retrieved_docs.content: This condition's predominant characteristic is anemia with varying HbH amounts (0.8% to 40%). The mutation type, whether deletional or nondeletional, affects clinical severity. Patients often present with severe microcytic or normocytic anemia early in life. Hemoglobin levels below 7 g/dL may be reported. MCH is also often low at less than 20 pg, and the peripheral blood film typically shows poikilocytosis with tear-drop cells, increased erythroblasts, and target cells. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: May-Hegglin Anomaly -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Review of complete blood count (CBC) and careful evaluation of peripheral blood smear along with a detailed family history of bleeding diathesis are key for establishing the diagnosis of MHA.

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Alpha Thalassemia -- Diagnostic Tests
retrieved_docs.content: Peripheral smear: This lab result shows microcytic, hypochromic anemia, target cells, teardrop cells, and cells with basophilic stippling in thalassemia cases, though these findings are also common in IDA. The presence of golf-ball-like hemoglobin inclusions indicates HbH disease.

retrieved_docs.title: Erythrocyte Inclusions -- Clinicopathologic Correlations -- Hemoglobin Inclusions
retrieved_docs.content: Inclusions of hemoglobin are abundant in the normoblasts of patients with beta-thalassemia and other thalassemias. [21] However, patients who have undergone splenectomy will also have increased numbers of these inclusions on the peripheral blood smear. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Hemoglobin C Disease -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Hemoglobin analysis reveals three different patterns of normal hemoglobin in an adult. These are Hb A (95 to 98%) containing two alpha and two beta chains, Hb A2 (2% to 3%) containing two alpha and two delta chains, and Hb F (less than 1%) containing two alpha and two gamma chains.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Hemoglobin Synthesis -- Testing
retrieved_docs.content: CBC includes measurement of hemoglobin level in the blood. Normal hemoglobin concentrations are approximately 13.5 to 18.0 grams per deciliter in men and 11.5 to 16.0 grams per deciliter in women. CBC also measures the size of erythrocytes through the mean corpuscular volume (MCV). Low MCV, also known as microcytosis, is often the first indicator of thalassemia or thalassemia trait. A peripheral blood smear can also detect suspected hematologic disorders. Providers can see sizes, colors, and variations in the shape of erythrocytes. Peripheral smears can detect nuclei in erythrocytes, which are abnormal and signify pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2812
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia, which is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, is a hereditary hemoglobin disorder that can precipitate serious respiratory complications in adults. Clinically, داء الكريات المنجلية is associated with respiratory failure, respiratory insufficiency, and adult cardiac arrest, underscoring the need for vigilant respiratory monitoring and prompt intervention in patients affected by this disease.

Recognizing these interconnections, the associated syndromes, and the relevant laboratory parameters (e.g., total hemoglobin) is essential for accurate diagnosis, interpretation of test results, and targeted management of infants during the neonatal and early infancy periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1802
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, and this hemoglobin disorder commonly manifests as نقص الأكسجين في الدم, indicating reduced oxygen levels in the blood.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2762
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, and داء الكريات المنجلية is clinically associated with قساح.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 934
retrieved_docs.content: that warrant careful ophthalmologic monitoring in patients with thalassemic disorders. Overall, the interrelationships among chronic kidney disease–related anemia, various deficiency and hemolytic anemias, thalassemia, and related immune‑mediated conditions underscore the importance of integrated diagnostic and therapeutic strategies that address both hematologic abnormalities and their associated systemic manifestations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2605
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell retinopathy (اعتلال الشبكية بالخلايا المنجلية) is clinically associated with thalassemia (الثلاسيميا). Both disorders are hemoglobinopathies that can coexist in the same patient, and the presence of thalassemia may influence the ocular manifestations observed in sickle cell disease. Recognizing this association underscores the importance of comprehensive ophthalmic assessment and coordinated care for individuals affected by either or both conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2304
retrieved_docs.content: Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage is classified as spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2774
retrieved_docs.content: Sickle cell anemia is classified as داء الكريات المنجلية, the Arabic term for sickle cell disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2441
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Abnormal” is used as an interpretation for both the Arabic phrase “البزل السّلوي غير الطبيعي” and the condition “فقر دم فانكوني.” In clinical practice, describing a spinal tap as “البزل السّلوي غير الطبيعي” indicates an abnormal cerebrospinal fluid result, while labeling “فقر دم فانكوني” as abnormal reflects the presence of Fanconi anemia, a hereditary bone‑marrow failure syndrome. Recognizing that “Abnormal” serves as a common descriptor for these distinct entities highlights its role as a generic marker of pathological or atypical findings in patient evaluation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1053
retrieved_docs.content: Other conditions or status of the mother complicating pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium is a broad diagnostic category that includes COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY. Within this category, a specific manifestation is Spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester, which is further classified as Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage. Recognizing that spotting in an unspecified trimester represents an early‑trimester hemorrhagic event and is considered a pregnancy‑related complication helps clinicians organize maternal conditions that affect pregnancy, delivery, or the puerperium and guides appropriate assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 4 -- Community 2884
retrieved_docs.content: The neonatal disorder (اضطراب لدى حديث الولادة) includes as a specific manifestation the condition of fetal erythroblastosis (كثرة الأرومات الحمر الجنينية), indicating that fetal erythroblastosis is a particular subtype or child entity within the broader category of neonatal disorders. Recognizing this relationship is important for accurate diagnosis and targeted management of affected newborns.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2004
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Abnormal” is used as an interpretation of فقر دم فانكوني (Fanconi anemia), indicating that the clinical and laboratory findings associated with this genetic disorder are considered abnormal. Fanconi anemia is a hereditary condition characterized by bone‑marrow failure, congenital anomalies, and a heightened risk of malignancies, and the descriptor “Abnormal” reflects the pathological nature of its presentations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 1 -- Community 218
retrieved_docs.content: the diagnostic realm, the term “Abnormal” is used as an interpretation for both abnormal amniocentesis (البزل السّلوي غير الطبيعي) and Fanconi anemia (فقر دم فانكوني), indicating that the presence of atypical findings in amniotic fluid analysis or hematologic evaluation is flagged as abnormal. Overall, these relationships illustrate a structured taxonomy in obstetrics: broad pregnancy complications encompass specific anesthesia‑related maternal morbidities, fluid and membrane disorders, and labor‑related conditions; each of these entities is further classified into more precise diagnostic or outcome categories, facilitating comprehensive patient assessment and targeted management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 4 -- Community 2869
retrieved_docs.content: الشعور بالضعف is clinically associated with فقر الدم, indicating that patients with anemia commonly experience a sensation of weakness.
